title: BEM-924 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM924_WORKER_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-924 WORKER FINAL WORKING SYSTEM PROTOCOL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS
Findings:
- Operator complaint is valid. Previous BEM-919..BEM-923 protocols each improved one layer but did not provide a single final implementation protocol.
- The final protocol must be one implementation map, not a sequence of isolated clarifications.
- The document must start with fact/current-state diagnosis, then target working architecture, then executable roadmap, then prompt appendix, then release criteria.
- Telegram is one module only. Provider model is one module only. Prompts are one module only. The goal is a working managing system.
- The protocol must integrate Claude requirements as acceptance criteria, not as comments.
- The protocol must explicitly state what is autonomous, what is operator-gated, and what is release-blocking.
Required final roadmap structure:
BEM-925: SSOT foundation: elements_registry, contours_registry, providers_registry, provider policies, prompt templates, object refs.
BEM-926: Core workflow hardening: concurrency, release_proof_manifest, non-null CI/Git SHA, proof policy, fixed re-audit checklist.
BEM-927: Dispatch and managed channel runtime: provider-aware queue lifecycle, auto-trigger decision, managed channel consumer, event log transitions.
BEM-928: Object and contour execution lifecycle: curator/object runners, internal contour algorithm, provider-aware failover, role reports.
BEM-929: Telegram operator interface: input envelope, mapping to managed channel, GD Curator primary route, optional curator routes, canonical reports, report_period_config owner OPERATOR.
BEM-930: E2E operational tests: mock Telegram command -> GD Curator -> DIR -> WRK -> provider-aware contour -> proof -> canonical report; mock_status and production_status split.
BEM-931: Production operator gate: secret names, webhook, schedule/daemon model, issue #31 permission, paid API/LLM runtime, real pilot choice.
BEM-932: Claude re-audit package and release decision: bundle checklist, evidence, gaps, PASS/CONDITIONAL/FAIL.
Definition of working system:
- A task from operator can enter the system through approved interface.
- It is converted to envelope and routed to GD Curator.
- It can move vertically through curators and horizontally between contours.
- Logical roles are executed by selected providers with failover evidence.
- Every transition is written to event log/managed channel/dispatch state.
- Every PASS has proof and non-null CI/Git SHA for release.
- Operator receives canonical report at configured period or on demand.
- Claude can re-audit evidence without relying on GPT self-claims.
Prompt requirements:
- Include content drafts for object prompts, role templates, contour overlays and operational contracts.
- Prompts must reference registries and not duplicate all rules.
- No local element rules by default.
Operator gates:
- Real secrets/tokens, production webhook mutation, new schedule trigger, always-on daemon, issue #31 write permission, paid API, LLM runtime, real pilot/client/scope/metrics.
Status recommendation:
- FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS.
No issue comments.
